guideline_references: ESC 2018 Guidelines for the diagnosis and management of syncope
CSANZ / NHFA guidance on the assessment of transient loss of consciousness
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present clinician who took vitals, performed a full neurological and cardiovascular examination, orthostatic vitals and inferred a tilt-table result. Breath-Ezy CANNOT examine, auscultate or measure vitals without a device, so the true tier is an urgent same-day IN-PERSON assessment (T3) even though the final diagnosis is benign — the dangerous mimics (cardiac/arrhythmic syncope, seizure, occult head injury from the fall) cannot be excluded remotely.

Premature-closure / false-reassurance trap: the presentation is a textbook benign reflex faint, so the tempting error is to reassure and discharge to home (under-triage). A first transient loss of consciousness with a witnessed collapse and a fall warrants same-day in-person evaluation regardless of how classic the history sounds.

Head-injury safety-net: the patient fell during the event; the correct descent includes a delayed-intracranial-bleed watch (severe headache, repeated vomiting, drowsiness, focal deficit) that escalates to 000.
Age 24 inferred as adult from stated age.
Source category: Cardiovascular (CVD), source note CDV-047.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
